BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin after the Stoneridge memorial-quilt commission came in. Checking whether the quilting side covers its own materials before craft-fair season.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Quilting nets ~$5k/yr on top of the legal-aid salary; revisit after holiday craft fairs.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Iverson)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Iverson said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is ~2-3 quilts/month at current material costs. With the $1,800 Stoneridge commission plus steady pattern-PDF sales there's plenty of headroom. Main concern is winter dip but even at 1 quilt/month the materials are covered.
ActionItems: Confirm Cotton Patch bulk-cotton pricing before the spring restock
Revisit after holiday craft fairs to true up 2025 quilting actuals
